text_original: Heaven and earth do not act from (the impulse of) any wish to be benevolent; they deal with all things as the dogs of grass are dealt with. The sages do not act from (any wish to be) benevolent; they deal with the people as the dogs of grass are dealt with. May not the space between heaven and earth be compared to a bellows?
text_normalized: Heaven and earth do not act from (the impulse of) any wish to be benevolent; they deal with all things as the dogs of grass are dealt with. The sages do not act from (any wish to be) benevolent; they deal with the people as the dogs of grass are dealt with. May not the space between heaven and earth be compared to a bellows?

text_original: 'Tis emptied, yet it loses not its power;
text_normalized: 'Tis emptied, yet it loses not its power;

text_original: 'Tis moved again, and sends forth air the more.
text_normalized: 'Tis moved again, and sends forth air the more.

text_original: Much speech to swift exhaustion lead we see;
text_normalized: Much speech to swift exhaustion lead we see;

text_original: Your inner being guard, and keep it free.
text_normalized: Your inner being guard, and keep it free.
